---
title: "Instructor Guide"
subtitle: "{{< var track.title >}}"
---

::: {.callout-warning}
This page and the linked instructor notes are part of the **instructor build** and are
not intended for learner distribution. Do not share the instructor site URL with participants.
:::

## Track overview for instructors

<!-- Brief notes on the overall design intent of this track. Why were these five episodes
     chosen? What story are they telling together? -->

## Suggested schedule

<!-- Adapt this for your delivery context (half-day, full-day, multi-day, async, etc.) -->

| Time | Episode | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| 0:00 | Welcome + Setup check | 15 min |
| 0:15 | Episode 1 | ~60 min |
| 1:15 | Break | 15 min |
| 1:30 | Episode 2 | ~60 min |
| ... | ... | ... |

## Episode-level instructor notes

| Episode | Notes |
|---------|-------|
| [Episode 1](notes-01.qmd) | Link to per-episode notes |
| [Episode 2](notes-02.qmd) | |
| [Episode 3](notes-03.qmd) | |
| [Episode 4](notes-04.qmd) | |
| [Episode 5](notes-05.qmd) | |

## Adapting this track

These materials are released under {{< var license >}} and may be freely adapted.
If you teach a modified version, please:

1. Keep the Carpentries acknowledgement in the footer.
2. Update `_variables.yml` with your name, institution, and delivery date.
3. Consider opening a pull request if you fix a bug or add an improvement that
   would benefit others.

## Known issues and gaps

<!-- Document any known errors, outdated content, or gaps in coverage here.
     This helps future instructors and contributors. -->

- NA (update as issues are found)

## Carpentries pedagogical framework

This track follows the lesson design principles described in the
[Carpentries Curriculum Development Handbook](https://cdh.carpentries.org), in particular:

- Backward design: episodes are built from learning objectives, not from content outlines.
- Formative assessment through exercises placed immediately after new concepts.
- Learning objectives use Bloom's taxonomy action verbs.
- Key points at the end of each episode reinforce consolidation.

For a deeper orientation to these principles, the handbook is highly recommended reading
before teaching this material for the first time.
